description: You are assisting a hazmat coordinator at an industrial park incident where five chemicals have been simultaneously released. Using CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), assess the vapor cloud behavior for Chlorine (CAS 7782-50-5), Ammonia (CAS 7664-41-7), Phosgene (CAS 75-44-5), Methyl isocyanate (CAS 624-83-9), and Sulfur dioxide (CAS 7446-09-5). For each chemical, record the vapor density (relative to air=1), boiling point, and water solubility. Include a 'Cloud Behavior Classification' ('Ground-Level Hazard' if density > 1.0, 'Elevated Disperser' if < 1.0, or 'Near-Neutral' if ~1.0) and a 'Rain Scrubbing' status ('Rain-scrubbable' if soluble, 'Not rain-scrubbable' if insoluble or reacts violently). Save the assessment, formatted with a section for each chemical, to ~/Desktop/cloud_behavior_assessment.txt.
